items.title: Nvidia says its AI data center design runs hotter to use a lot less water
items.summary: Public pushback against data centers has emphasized their water and energy consumption, and now Nvidia is highlighting its claim that the Rubin generation reference design for a fully liquid-cooled data center has "eliminated massive amounts of power usage and pretty much all water usage." Still, it doesn't address all of the concerns around AI data […]

items.title: Google invests in A24 to build AI movie tools
items.summary: Google's DeepMind AI lab is teaming up with A24 to develop new movie production technologies that aim to help future filmmakers "expand their storytelling possibilities." As part of this new research and development collaboration, The Wall Street Journal reports that Google is investing "around $75 million" into A24, marking the first time the search giant […]

items.title: Daybreak: Tools for securing every organization in the world
items.summary: OpenAI introduces new Daybreak tools, including Codex Security and GPT-5.5-Cyber, to help organizations find, validate, and patch vulnerabilities at scale.

items.title: Patch the Planet: a Daybreak initiative to support open source maintainers
items.summary: OpenAI introduces Patch the Planet, a Daybreak initiative helping open-source maintainers find, validate, and fix vulnerabilities with AI and expert review.

items.title: Samsung Electronics brings ChatGPT and Codex to employees
items.summary: Samsung Electronics deploys ChatGPT Enterprise and Codex to employees worldwide, marking one of OpenAI’s largest enterprise AI rollouts.

items.title: AI is cursing renters with the promise of impossible homes
items.summary: Joyce, a native New Yorker, didn't think finding her first solo apartment in the city would be easy. But she also didn't think it'd be "hell." After looking at a lot of tiny, overpriced places she described as "shitholes," Joyce found her dream apartment: a reasonably priced studio in Manhattan. "It was big and airy, […]

items.title: Valve explains why it isn’t subsidizing the Steam Machine
items.summary: Valve finally announced the price of the Steam Machine, and like a lot of new gadgets these days, it's not cheap: It starts at $1,049 for a 512GB model, and a 2TB model costs $300 more. Configurations with a bundled Steam Controller cost an extra $79 each. Despite Valve offering a console alternative with the […]

items.title: Can anyone join Musk in the trillionaire club? Zuckerberg has best shot, according to prediction markets
items.summary: The Meta CEO is seen as next to join the Tesla and SpaceX CEO. Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang is seen as the second-most-likely.

items.title: China imposes trade curbs on dozens of U.S. firms in retaliation for Pentagon blacklist
items.summary: The Pentagon earlier this month updated its so-called 1260H list by adding a slew of Chinese technology companies to a roster of entities it believes have aided Beijing's military.

items.title: Beijing is summoning executives again, but here's why that's causing less worry than in 2021
items.summary: Beijing is summoning tech executives for meetings as it did in 2021, but now it has less room to wield a heavy hand due to deflation and rivalry with the U.S.

items.title: China's import of custard apples is sparking fears in Taiwan
items.summary: Taiwan's agriculture ministry is worried that Beijing wants to weaponise a local specialty: the atemoya.

items.title: US eases oil sanctions as Iran denies Vance claim on nuclear inspectors

items.title: Magnificent Messi makes history, breaks all-time World Cup scoring record
items.summary: Messi scored a record-setting 17th World Cup goal in Argentina's game against Austria. And, then, he netted another goal, making him the all-time leader in World Cup tournaments (men and women).

items.title: Former Kenyan justice minister blocked from entering Uganda, lawyers' body says
items.summary: Martha Karua is a lawyer representing detained Ugandan opposition leader Kizza Besigye who is on trial for treason.

items.title: Did you know? Alan Greenspan and Ayn Rand were close friends
items.summary: One of the most intellectually important relationships in the life of the late Fed chair Alan Greenspan was with his close friend, the formidable novelist and libertarian thinker Ayn Rand.
